Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: this is a detailed and well structured process description but as an analyst I know there are always gaps between the ideal process and how it actually works

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the interviewee gave a clear linear overview but real world processes have dependencies exceptions and human judgment calls that need unpacking

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: I should focus on asking questions that reveal the hidden complexities

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the goal is to understand not just the what but the why and how behind each step

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Ill organize my questions around key themes process boundaries decision logic communication flows exception handling and success metrics

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: for process boundaries I need to know what triggers this whole workflow and what officially ends it

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: the handoff points between roles are particularly important to map

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: decision criteria need to be concrete depends on workload is vague so Ill ask for specific thresholds or scoring systems

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: communication patterns matter because they reveal coordination pain points

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: exception handling is where processes often break down so I need examples of common deviations

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: finally understanding how they measure success will show what they truly value in this process

Name: Validation - PE
Text: the questions should be open ended to encourage detailed explanations not yes no answers

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Ill avoid technical implementation details and stay at the conceptual level as requested
